title: rhinanthus coccineus
intro: Rhinanthus coccineus, also known as the red monkey flower or red rattlebox, is a perennial plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Rhinanthus coccineus has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune system function.
